title: Scribbles: Inverted curve (horizontal) — retry, continuous shading
restrictions: RETRY of #1185 after round 22 was rejected ('not a scribble, nothing interesting, change technique'). New technique = `scribble` mode 'shade': ONE continuous coiling line that sweeps along an inverted-curve arch while zig-zagging across its thickness with a loop at each turn (real scribble motion), so a volumetric tonal FORM emerges (denser/darker at the crown) — a readable object, not scattered marks or an even texture. Open negative space around the arch. Monochrome; hand-made continuity + tonal gradient (the traits the user has liked). Density kept from going fully solid (paper-rip). The 16 span arch height/width, ribbon thickness, crown swell (volume), coil density (tone), loop size, passes, and hand energy.
